name: Salt-Hound

tame.$comment: instinct effect magnitudes/radii are invented (VILLAGER-AND-GODHEAD-SPEC Part III gives the fantasy — 'damage mult', 'range grows', 'hits briefly pin' — but no numbers). Fangs +20% dmg; Blood-Scent +50% growl-radar range; Deathgrip pins 1.2s, first-guess. behavior mirrors arms-warrior's engageRange/breakHpPct verbatim per the spec's instruction ('fighter kit off its own level... in line with the Arms warrior behavior numbers').

instincts.name: Blood-Scent
instincts.text: The dried sea keeps no secrets from a nose it made — the growl finds trouble from further off.

instincts.name: Deathgrip
instincts.text: It doesn't let go right away. Neither should you.

text: Runs in threes. The white coat isn't fur.

name: Scuttle-Crab

tame.$comment: instinct effect magnitudes/radii are invented (spec gives the fantasy, not numbers). Deep Pockets is the spec's own '+2 more carry' verbatim. Old Barnacle: hound aggro x0.7 within 6m, first-guess ('walking calm', no radius given). Tide-Shell has no magnitude — it's a boolean guard the presentation layer reads (player-only, once per day), same shape as arms talents with a params flag.

instincts.name: Deep Pockets
instincts.text: It found room. It always finds room.

instincts.name: Tide-Shell
instincts.text: Once a day, the shell remembers it isn't just its own to guard.

instincts.name: Old Barnacle
instincts.text: Something this old and this calm makes even a hound think twice.

text: Mostly harmless. Excellent soup. Old Ghal is watching how you phrase that.

name: Dust-Eel
text: The flats look flat. The flats are not flat.

name: Flats Raider
text: Desperate, organized, and takeable — subdue instead of slay and the yoke-post has a customer.

name: Old Shellback
bossNotes: A crab the size of a chapel wearing a hull as a shell. Teaches the boss grammar: preparation (fire the barnacle clusters), arena (the wreck-ring), weak-point (the old harpoon scar — a whaler put it there and didn't live to write a fourth verse; Shellback keeps what beats him, which is why the verse is in his hoard). Band-1 keystone: his death opens the wreck-ring's salvage and, someday, the descent. Killing him removes the flats' apex — hound packs grow. He is not evil; he is the world's thesis: endure by wearing the wreckage of what you lost.
text: 'Shellback' is old sailor-talk for a mariner who crossed the equator — an initiated salt of the deep water. He is the oldest sailor left, still wearing his ship home. The village children say 'Old Shipback,' and no priest has the heart to correct them.

name: Eel-Wolf
$comment: M3.a review against CRAFT-AND-BUILD-SPEC's B2 enemy-damage rule ('B2 hostiles hit ~2x B1'): salt-hound (B1 pack-hunter baseline) hits for 8 -> the 2x band is 16, exactly this creature's existing damage. No change needed; hp 70 vs the hound's 40 is the spec's own headroom for the armor ladder underneath it. M3.b adds the tame block (REEF-FOREST-SPEC/CRAFT-AND-BUILD-SPEC Part 3: 'eel-wolf joins tame tier 2, data only') — same tier/behavior shape as the salt-hound (its B1 tame-tier-2 template), craves item-urchin-meat (the reef's own B2 tier-0 food, in place of the hound's crab-meat), meals 3 unchanged from the hound. Instinct effect types are the SAME generic hooks beast_instinct_mult/_duration already read off any creature's tame.instincts (damage-mult/growl-range-mult/pin-on-hit) — reusing them means the eel-wolf 'just works' through the existing beast_strike/beast_growl_tick code with zero new code, per the brief's own instruction ('data only'). Names/flavor are original, not a hound reskin.

instincts.name: Riptooth
instincts.text: It bites like the current means it.

instincts.name: Eel-Sense
instincts.text: It feels the reef move before you hear it — the growl finds trouble from further off.

instincts.name: Lockjaw
instincts.text: The old eel half of it never learned to let go.

text: What the reef made when it had to choose between its two best ideas.

name: Urchin-Back
$comment: new archetype (M3.a): harmless until struck, then a slow pursuit (enemy.gd: peaceful like 'ambient' until on_hit() sets provoked, then behaves like any other chaser at its own slow speed). hp/damage are first guesses — modest, an armored crab-analog: hp above scuttle-crab's 15 (the shell earns something), damage low and roughly half a hound's, well under the B2 x2 law since it is never the aggressor.
text: Mostly harmless, if you leave it be. It has never once started anything.

name: The Drowned
text: Dead sailors who don't know the sea left. They walk up out of the low places at night, still doing their work. Laying one to rest is a calling; there are kinder verbs than 'kill'.

name: Angler-Stalker
text: A friendly light in a dark place. The game's face: it might be safety, and it might not.

name: The Anglermother
bossNotes: PLACEHOLDER remnant id — needs own remnant item. Fight in the dark; her lure is the only light and you need it to see. She has been calling your tribesmen in their dreams — village dread events foreshadow her for days.
text: The light in the deep wood.

name: The Drowned Priest
bossNotes: Not (only) a fight. The All-Tide's last high priest, holding a three-generation rite. Reaching him is the EA climax and the Verdict fork: join the rite, sever it with Ur-Noth's blessing, or take the thread. L3 lore lands here.
text: He has been waiting longer than your family has existed. He is not surprised to see you.
